mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index values indicate inflationary pressure that forces discount rate expectations higher, compressing equity valuations and generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 or positive 20-day returns occur in over 65% of high-inflation months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration that triggers immediate risk-off positioning and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered months or the signal shows no statistical separation from baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and builder confidence that sustains corporate earnings growth and drives positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60-day returns in over 60% of cases or interaction term loses significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects capacity constraints that suppress profit margins and generate negative monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses to positive during tightening periods or IC drops below zero over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing total nonfarm payrolls demonstrates expanding economic activity that supports corporate revenue forecasts and produces positive short-term market momentum.
failure_condition: Positive payroll revisions correlate with negative 5-day returns in over 58% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates combined with rising real GDP growth create an accommodative financial environment that boosts equity risk premiums and sustains positive quarterly returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 2% annually over backtested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in real personal consumption expenditures exceeding 0.8% signals weakening household spending that directly reduces corporate earnings expectations and triggers negative monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: Negative consumption shocks fail to predict negative 20-day returns with a p-value below 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicates rapid capacity utilization that fuels short-term earnings revisions and positive weekly equity returns.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power when applied outside recovery regimes or yields negative alpha in walk-forward validation.
